content.content:
1	name: Nightly upstream merge
2	
3	# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
4	# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
5	# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
6	# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
7	# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the dependency gates, then
8	# (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) an Opus review of the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
9	# conflicted — against the fork feature manifest (a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
10	# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
11	# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
12	# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
13	# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
14	# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
15	# untouched -> recovery loop.
16	#
17	# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
18	# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
19	#
20	# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
21	# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
22	
23	on:
24	  schedule:
25	    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
26	  workflow_dispatch:
27	    inputs:
28	      force_tag:
29	        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
30	        required: false
31	        default: ''
32	
33	permissions:
34	  contents: write
35	
36	# (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Never let two nightly instances interleave: an overlapping
37	# schedule + manual dispatch can delete/replace the mutable candidate branch
38	# between one run's merge, build checkout and advance. Queue, never cancel — a
39	# running merge/build must finish or fail on its own.
40	concurrency:
41	  group: nightly-merge
42	  cancel-in-progress: false
43	
44	jobs:
45	  merge:
46	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
47	    outputs:
48	      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
49	      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
50	      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
51	      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
52	      sha: ${{ steps.do.outputs.sha }}
53	    env:
54	      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
55	      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
56	      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
57	      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
58	      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
59	    steps:
60	      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
61	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
62	        with:
63	          fetch-depth: 0
64	
65	      - name: Setup Node.js
66	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
67	        with:
68	          node-version: '22'
69	
70	      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
71	      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
72	      # frozen build install consumes.
73	      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
74	        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
75	        with:
76	          bun-version: '1.3.14'
77	
78	      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
79	        id: do
80	        shell: bash
81	        run: |
82	          set -euo pipefail
83	          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
84	          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"
85	
86	          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
87	          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
88	          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
89	          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
90	          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
91	          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
92	          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
93	          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
94	          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
95	            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
96	          FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'
97	          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
98	            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
99	          else
100	            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
101	            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
102	          fi
103	          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
104	          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
105	          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
106	            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
107	            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
108	            exit 0
109	          fi
110	          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
111	            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
112	          fi
113	
114	          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
115	          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
116	          git switch -c "$CAND"
117	          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
118	          set +e
119	          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
120	          set -e
121	
122	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) `set +e` above accepts EVERY nonzero merge exit,
123	          # not just "conflicts". A fatal merge failure (lock error, corrupt
124	          # object, OOM) leaves NO MERGE_HEAD and possibly no conflicts either —
125	          # without this check it would read as a clean merge and we'd commit a
126	          # single-parent non-merge with a bumped baseline.
127	          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
128	            echo "::error::git merge of $NEWTAG failed fatally (no MERGE_HEAD) — aborting. Baseline untouched."
129	            exit 1
130	          fi
131	
132	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) Surface — never silently discard — upstream's
133	          # workflow-tree delta before we drop it: upstream sometimes moves
134	          # build-relevant logic INTO CI (a codegen step, a required env var).
135	          # The delta goes to the job summary + log and is handed to the
136	          # semantic review below so build-relevant CI changes get flagged.
137	          WFDELTA_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/upstream-workflow-delta.diff"
138	          : > "$WFDELTA_FILE"
139	          if git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
140	            git diff "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows > "$WFDELTA_FILE" || true
141	          fi
142	          if [ -s "$WFDELTA_FILE" ]; then
143	            {
144	              echo "## Upstream workflow-tree delta ($BASE → $NEWTAG) — DROPPED, .github/workflows is fork-owned"
145	              echo '```'
146	              git diff --name-status "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows
147	              echo '```'
148	              echo "Full patch in the job log (group: dropped upstream workflow delta). Port anything build-relevant by hand."
149	            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
150	            echo "::group::dropped upstream workflow delta"
151	            cat "$WFDELTA_FILE"
152	            echo "::endgroup::"
153	          fi
154	
155	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows is FORK-OWNED: the fork
156	          # carries ZERO upstream workflow files (upstream CI targets upstream's
157	          # infra/secrets and is meaningless here), and the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN
158	          # can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
159	          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
160	          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
161	          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
162	          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
163	          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
164	          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
165	          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
166	          # workflow path it could resurrect.
167	          #
168	          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
169	          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
170	          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
171	          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
172	          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
173	          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
174	          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
175	          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
176	            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
177	              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
178	            else
179	              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
180	            fi
181	          done
182	          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows
183	
184	          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
185	          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
186	            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
187	            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
188	            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
189	            attempt=1
190	            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
191	              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
192	              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
193	              echo "::endgroup::"
194	              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
195	              attempt=$((attempt+1))
196	            done
197	            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
198	            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
199	              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
200	              exit 1
201	            fi
202	          else
203	            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
204	          fi
205	
206	          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
207	          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
208	          MISSING=0
209	          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
210	          awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
211	            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
212	            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
213	              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
214	            else
215	              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
216	              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
217	            fi
218	          done
219	          echo "::endgroup::"
220	          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
221	            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
222	            exit 1
223	          fi
224	
225	          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
226	          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
227	          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
228	          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
229	          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
230	          # runner spins up), not here:
231	          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
232	          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
233	          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
234	          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
235	          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
236	          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
237	          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
238	          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
239	          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
240	          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
241	          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
242	          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
243	          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
244	          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
245	          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
246	          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
247	          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
248	          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
249	          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
250	          echo "::endgroup::"
251	
252	          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
253	          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
254	          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
255	          set +e
256	          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
257	          rc=$?
258	          set -e
259	          cat "$LOCKLOG"
260	          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
261	            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
262	              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
263	            else
264	              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
265	            fi
266	            exit 1
267	          fi
268	          echo "::endgroup::"
269	
270	          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
271	          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
272	          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
273	          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
274	          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
275	          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
276	          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
277	          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
278	          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
279	          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
280	          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
281	          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
282	          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
283	          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
284	          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
285	          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
286	          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
287	          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
288	          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
289	          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
290	          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
291	          attempt=1
292	          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
293	            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
294	            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
295	            echo "::endgroup::"
296	            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
297	            attempt=$((attempt+1))
298	          done
299	          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
300	          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
301	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
302	            exit 1
303	          fi
304	          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
305	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
306	            exit 1
307	          fi
308	          echo "--- review verdict ---"
309	          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
310	          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
311	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
312	            exit 1
313	          fi
314	          echo "::endgroup::"
315	
316	          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
317	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
318	          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
319	          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
320	          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
321	          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
322	          # untouched).
323	          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
324	            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
325	            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
326	            exit 1
327	          fi
328	          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
329	          git add -A
330	          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
331	          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
332	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
333	          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
334	          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
335	          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
336	          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
337	          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
338	          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
339	          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
340	          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
341	          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
342	          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
343	          git push origin "$CAND"
344	
345	          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
346	          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
347	          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
348	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
349	          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
350	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
351	          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
352	          # advanced unnoticed.
353	          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
354	          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."
355	
356	  build:
357	    needs: merge
358	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
359	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
360	    with:
361	      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
362	      # branch mutation between jobs.
363	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
364	      publish: true
365	
366	  advance:
367	    needs: [merge, build]
368	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
369	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
370	    steps:
371	      - name: Checkout
372	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
373	        with:
374	          fetch-depth: 0
375	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
376	        shell: bash
377	        run: |
378	          set -euo pipefail
379	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
380	          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
381	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
382	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
383	          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
384	            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
385	            exit 1
386	          fi
387	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
388	          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
389	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
390	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."
391

file.content:
name: Nightly upstream merge

# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the dependency gates, then
# (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) an Opus review of the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
# conflicted — against the fork feature manifest (a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
# untouched -> recovery loop.
#
# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
#
# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      force_tag:
        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
        required: false
        default: ''

permissions:
  contents: write

# (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Never let two nightly instances interleave: an overlapping
# schedule + manual dispatch can delete/replace the mutable candidate branch
# between one run's merge, build checkout and advance. Queue, never cancel — a
# running merge/build must finish or fail on its own.
concurrency:
  group: nightly-merge
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
      sha: ${{ steps.do.outputs.sha }}
    env:
      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
      # frozen build install consumes.
      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: '1.3.14'

      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
        id: do
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"

          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
          FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'
          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
          else
            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
          fi
          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
          fi

          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
          git switch -c "$CAND"
          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
          set +e
          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
          set -e

          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) `set +e` above accepts EVERY nonzero merge exit,
          # not just "conflicts". A fatal merge failure (lock error, corrupt
          # object, OOM) leaves NO MERGE_HEAD and possibly no conflicts either —
          # without this check it would read as a clean merge and we'd commit a
          # single-parent non-merge with a bumped baseline.
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::git merge of $NEWTAG failed fatally (no MERGE_HEAD) — aborting. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi

          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) Surface — never silently discard — upstream's
          # workflow-tree delta before we drop it: upstream sometimes moves
          # build-relevant logic INTO CI (a codegen step, a required env var).
          # The delta goes to the job summary + log and is handed to the
          # semantic review below so build-relevant CI changes get flagged.
          WFDELTA_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/upstream-workflow-delta.diff"
          : > "$WFDELTA_FILE"
          if git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            git diff "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows > "$WFDELTA_FILE" || true
          fi
          if [ -s "$WFDELTA_FILE" ]; then
            {
              echo "## Upstream workflow-tree delta ($BASE → $NEWTAG) — DROPPED, .github/workflows is fork-owned"
              echo '```'
              git diff --name-status "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows
              echo '```'
              echo "Full patch in the job log (group: dropped upstream workflow delta). Port anything build-relevant by hand."
            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            echo "::group::dropped upstream workflow delta"
            cat "$WFDELTA_FILE"
            echo "::endgroup::"
          fi

          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows is FORK-OWNED: the fork
          # carries ZERO upstream workflow files (upstream CI targets upstream's
          # infra/secrets and is meaningless here), and the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN
          # can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
          # workflow path it could resurrect.
          #
          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
            else
              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
            fi
          done
          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows

          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          MISSING=0
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
          attempt=1
          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "--- review verdict ---"
          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
          # untouched).
          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
          # advanced unnoticed.
          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
      # branch mutation between jobs.
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
            exit 1
          fi
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. 332	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by

356	  build:
357	    needs: merge
358	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
359	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
360	    with:
361	      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
362	      # branch mutation between jobs.
363	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
364	      publish: true
365	
366	  advance:
367	    needs: [merge, build]
368	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
369	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
370	    steps:
371	      - name: Checkout
372	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
373	        with:
374	          fetch-depth: 0
375	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
376	        shell: bash
377	        run: |
378	          set -euo pipefail
379	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
380	          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
381	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
382	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
383	          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
384	            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
385	            exit 1
386	          fi
387	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
388	          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
389	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
390	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
          # advanced unnoticed.
          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
      # branch mutation between jobs.
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
            exit 1
          fi
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

content.text:
## My forward-looking review (Codex Ultra report to follow)

**Live run:** merge job in progress — past the point where the last two nights died (that crash hit at ~14s; it's now minutes in, meaning the fix held and Opus is resolving the 12 content conflicts).

**Why you've "never" had a green overnight:** each failure was a distinct one-shot incident class, fixed serially — LOCK-REGEN override drift → candidate-branch push deadlock (which masked the first fix) → first genuinely green scheduled nightly 2026-06-30 (v1.12.6 published unattended) → v1.14.3 workflow-push rejection → v1.15.0 modify/delete crash. Not one recurring bug; five different ones, each now with a specific fix in the workflow text.

**Verified fixed in current pipeline (I re-checked each mechanism is present):**
1. Workflow-push rejection → mid-merge restore + tree postcondition (line 323) + the merge commit's workflow tree always equals main's, so GitHub's `workflows`-permission check can't fire.
2. Modify/delete workflow crash → `261dc2d82`, repro-validated against a synthetic conflict; handles all unmerged shapes (modify/modify, add/add, rename) via the exists-in-HEAD test.
3. Candidate-branch deadlock → delete-before-push (line 342); a failed build can no longer poison future nights.
4. Override/lock drift → `check-override-consistency.mjs` + lock regen on the build's exact pinned bun, both in the cheap linux job before the ARM64 runner spins up.
5. Fatal-merge-reads-as-clean → MERGE_HEAD check.
6. Run interleaving → concurrency queue + immutable-SHA handoff between merge/build/advance.

**Remaining failure classes — all fail-closed (baseline untouched, next night retries), none wedge the pipeline:**
- **Self-healing, no action needed:** Anthropic API outage/rate limit (resolver or semantic review), `minimumReleaseAge` race on a <72h upstream dep pin, you pushing to main mid-run (advance refuses non-fast-forward; avoid pushing 05:00–07:00 UTC).
- **Manual by design (the gates working as intended):** Opus can't resolve a conflict in 3 attempts; semantic gate finds real feature BREAKAGE; genuine upstream build breakage (REFERR-GATE / natives). These *should* stop the pipeline.
- **One hygiene note (not a failure):** resolver-created untracked scratch files would be swept into `git add -A` — review-run changes are guarded, resolver leftovers aren't. Cosmetic risk only.

**Verdict:** for a typical upstream release delta, the pipeline should now go green unattended. The one structural class that kept recurring — upstream touching workflow files the fork owns — is now closed for every conflict shape, with a postcondition proof before commit. Residual red nights should be transient (API/aging races, auto-retry) or legitimate stops.

Codex Ultra is grinding through the same review adversarially; I'll report its findings + the live run outcome when they land.

cd ~ && uv run --with "" python - <<'EOF' 2>/dev/null || python - <<'EOF2'
EOF2
import sqlite3, time
c = sqlite3.connect(r"C:\Users\khair\.superset\local.db")
c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
rows = c.execute("""
  SELECT b.terminal_id, b.agent_id, b.last_event_type, b.last_event_at, s.status
  FROM terminal_agent_bindings b LEFT JOIN terminal_sessions s ON s.id = b.terminal_id
  ORDER BY b.last_event_at DESC LIMIT 15""").fetchall()
now = time.time()*1000
for r in rows:
    age_h = (now - r["last_event_at"])/3600000
    print(f"{r['terminal_id'][:14]:14} {r['agent_id']:8} {r['last_event_type']:20} sess={r['status']} age={age_h:.1f}h")
EOF

import sqlite3, time
c = sqlite3.connect(r"C:\Users\khair\.superset\local.db")
c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
rows = c.execute("""
  SELECT b.terminal_id, b.agent_id, b.last_event_type, b.last_event_at, s.status
  FROM terminal_agent_bindings b LEFT JOIN terminal_sessions s ON s.id = b.terminal_id
  ORDER BY b.last_event_at DESC LIMIT 20""").fetchall()
now = time.time() * 1000
for r in rows:
    age_h = (now - r["last_event_at"]) / 3600000
    print(f"{r['terminal_id'][:16]:16} {r['agent_id']:8} {str(r['last_event_type']):22} sess={str(r['status']):8} age={age_h:.1f}h")

import sqlite3, time
c = sqlite3.connect(r"C:\Users\khair\.superset\local.db")
c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
rows = c.execute("""
  SELECT b.terminal_id, b.agent_id, b.last_event_type, b.last_event_at, s.status
  FROM terminal_agent_bindings b LEFT JOIN terminal_sessions s ON s.id = b.terminal_id
  ORDER BY b.last_event_at DESC LIMIT 20""").fetchall()
now = time.time() * 1000
for r in rows:
    age_h = (now - r["last_event_at"]) / 3600000
    print(f"{r['terminal_id'][:16]:16} {r['agent_id']:8} {str(r['last_event_type']):22} sess={str(r['status']):8} age={age_h:.1f}h")

content.content:
1	# Verdict
2	
3	No—not unconditionally.
4	
5	The new `261dc2d82` fix is correct for the Jul 13/14 `release-cli.yml` modify/delete conflict. A typical stable, numeric `desktop-v*` release is now reasonably expected to go green unattended, assuming:
6	
7	- conflicts are resolvable;
8	- dependencies are old enough and resolvable;
9	- required Windows ARM64 native artifacts exist;
10	- Claude, npm, GitHub, and the ARM runner are available;
11	- upstream has not changed build/output conventions.
12	
13	But several legitimate fail-closed blockers remain, plus a small number of low-probability races that can leave future nightlies unable to repair the published state automatically.
14	
15	Line notation below:
16	
17	- N: [nightly-merge.yml](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml:1>)
18	- B: [build-arm64.yml](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml:1>)
19	- F: [FEATURES.md](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/FEATURES.md:1>)
20	- M: [materialize-native-closure.sh](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/scripts/materialize-native-closure.sh:1>)
21	- R: [check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs:1>)
22	
23	## Historical fixes: verified
24	
25	| Incident | Verdict | Evidence |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| LOCK-REGEN | Fixed for the original Mastra failure | N70–76 pins Bun 1.3.14, matching B69–70. N249 runs `node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs`; N256 regenerates `bun.lock`. Mastra overrides are absent. Bot merge `33fc326a2` subsequently succeeded. |
28	| CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH | Fixed under normal branch permissions | N342 deletes the disposable candidate before N343 pushes it. Failed builds no longer poison the next ordinary run. Bot merge `33fc326a2` postdates the fix. |
29	| WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED | Fixed for normal workflow additions/edits | N182 restores the exact `HEAD` workflow tree; N323–327 proves no workflow delta remains. Bot merge `848414f7f` succeeded after this fix and has zero first-parent workflow-directory changes. |
30	| New `261dc2d82` modify/delete fix | Textually correct; not yet proven by a later successful nightly | N175–181 resolves each unmerged workflow path to `HEAD` if present, otherwise `git rm`; N182 then performs the no-overlay sweep. |
31	
32	### Scrutiny of `261dc2d82`
33	
34	For the actual v1.15.0 case, `release-cli.yml` is absent in fork `HEAD`, so:
35	
36	```bash
37	git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p"   # false
38	git rm -q -f -- "$p"        # clears the modify/delete U entry
39	git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows
40	```
41	
42	That is correct. The same logic handles ordinary content conflicts, add/add, either modify/delete direction, rename targets, and rename/delete. Delete/delete normally auto-resolves before this loop. `set -euo pipefail` at N82 means failure inside the piped `while` is not swallowed.
43	
44	Residual pathological cases:
45	
46	- N175 uses newline-delimited `git diff --name-only`, not NUL-delimited output. Git documents that unusual pathnames are quoted/munged without `-z`; tabs, newlines, quotes, backslashes, and some non-ASCII names can therefore reach `cat-file`/`rm` as the wrong literal path. Expected error: `pathspec ... did not match any files`. Safe abort, extremely unlikely for an upstream workflow filename. [Git diff documentation](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-diff)
47	- A directory/file conflict such as a workflow file colliding with an upstream directory can fail during parent/child checkout ordering with `Is a directory`, `Directory not empty`, or another unmerged-path error. Safe abort, extremely unlikely.
48	- A rename across the `.github/workflows` boundary can materialize a conflict destination outside that pathspec. It will go to the general AI resolver, not this deterministic fork-owned sweep.
49	
50	## Genuine wedges or poisoned-state classes
51	
52	| Failure class | Trigger and exact boundary | Expected result | Likelihood | Classification |
53	|---|---|---|---:|---|
54	| **Queued run starts from stale `main`** | Run B is triggered while run A is active. N60–63 has no explicit `ref`, so B later checks out its event SHA, not the newly advanced `main`. B re-merges and republishes the same tag, then N388 runs `git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"`. | Non-fast-forward rejection after B has replaced the release with its sibling merge. A’s baseline is already current, so the next nightly exits at N105 and does not repair the off-main release or stale candidate. | Low scheduled-only; material if manual retries overlap or a run lasts into another schedule | **Fails-broken; future no-op nightlies preserve the wrong release.** GitHub concurrency allows only one running and, by default, one pending run; checkout defaults to the event ref/SHA. [Concurrency docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/control-workflow-concurrency), [checkout behavior](https://github.com/actions/checkout) |
55	| **Standalone build races nightly publishing** | B14–20 makes manual `workflow_dispatch` publish by default, and B has no release concurrency. Two builds of the same version interleave B298–315. | `422 already_exists`, tag-SHA mismatch, or one build replacing the other. Worst case: nightly verifies, another build replaces the release, then nightly advances without rechecking it. | Low unless manual builds are used during nightly | **Fails-broken; baseline can advance with the wrong release.** |
56	| **Upstream tag/package version mismatch** | N345 reads package version but never compares it with `NEWTAG`; the output is unused. B56 and B297 derive the release tag solely from `apps/desktop/package.json.version`. | No error. A `desktop-v1.15.0` merge retaining package version `1.14.3` can delete/rebuild the `desktop-v1.14.3` release, then advance the baseline to `desktop-v1.15.0`. | Very low, but package conflicts make it plausible | **Silent poison; subsequent nightlies skip the missing correct release.** No `bun run check:versions` runs either. |
57	| **AI removes merge state** | The resolver at N192 has unrestricted Git access. Only remaining U paths are checked; `MERGE_HEAD` is never rechecked. An accidental `git merge --abort` or reset makes the conflict check pass. | N328 bumps the baseline and N330 creates a normal single-parent commit without the upstream merge. | Extremely low | **Silent poison; upstream tag is permanently recorded as merged when it was skipped.** |
58	| **Candidate deletion blocked** | A ruleset/protection rejects N342, but `|| true` suppresses it. N343 then pushes a new divergent same-tag merge over the stale candidate. | Repeated non-fast-forward or GH013 rejection every night. | Configuration-dependent | **Persistent wedge until branch state/policy changes.** |
59	| **Direct push to `main` blocked or default branch renamed** | N388 hardcodes `refs/heads/main`. Branch protection, required PRs/signatures, or a different default branch rejects/misdirects it. | GH006/GH013, protected-branch, or non-fast-forward error after release publication. | Configuration-dependent | **Persistent wedge; release is repeatedly published ahead of the baseline.** |
60	| **Immutable releases enabled** | B298–306 implements delete-and-recreate. GitHub immutable releases permanently reserve a tag name after deletion. | Later `gh release create` fails, commonly with HTTP 422/tag reuse failure. | Configuration-dependent | **Permanent wedge for that version.** [GitHub immutable releases](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/concepts/supply-chain-security/immutable-releases) |
61	| **Tag deletion protected or transiently fails** | B299 suppresses every tag-delete error. If the old tag remains, B306’s `--target` cannot retarget it; GitHub ignores `target_commitish` for an existing tag. | `422 already_exists`, or B312–314 reports old tag SHA versus built SHA. | Configuration-dependent/low transiently | **Persistent if policy-driven; normally retryable if transient.** [Release API semantics](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases) |
62	| **Same-name upstream tag is moved** | N105 compares only the tag name with the baseline before comparing object IDs. | Clean green no-op despite different upstream bytes. | Extremely low; upstream tags should be immutable | **Undetected permanently.** |
63	| **Tag is not numeric-stable** | N100 only accepts `desktop-v[0-9][0-9.]*`. Prerelease/suffixed tags are ignored; multiple releases between schedules collapse to the latest stable tag. | Green no-op or older stable tag selected. | Certain for prerelease tags | **Not a red run, but it does not satisfy literal “every `desktop-v*` tag.”** |
64	| **Schedule is delayed, dropped, or disabled** | Cron is N25 at minute zero, a documented high-load time. Public-repository schedules can also be disabled after 60 days without activity. | No run exists to report an error. | Low; inactivity case conditional | **Retry next day if dropped; persistent until re-enabled if disabled.** [GitHub schedule behavior](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/troubleshoot-workflows) |
65	
66	## Fail-closed classes
67	
68	These leave the baseline untouched. Ordinary build failures leave a candidate branch, but N342 normally removes it on the next run.
69	
70	| Failure class | Failing command/error | Likelihood | State |
71	|---|---|---:|---|
72	| Upstream fetch/setup outage | N94–95 `git fetch`; setup actions N61/66/74 | Low each | Clean abort; automatic retry |
73	| Tag already incorporated but baseline pointer differs | `git merge --no-commit --no-ff` reports already up to date and creates no `MERGE_HEAD`; N127–129 calls it fatal | Very low | Safe but repeats until baseline is corrected |
74	| Ordinary unresolved code conflicts | N192 attempts Opus three times; N197–200 emits `Opus could not resolve all conflicts` | Medium on large/cross-cutting releases | Safe; may require manual recovery |
75	| Pathological workflow conflict | N177, N179, or N182 returns pathspec/unmerged/D-F error | Extremely low | Safe persistent abort |
76	| Marker loss | N212–222 emits `feature marker MISSING` | Low unless upstream touches fork areas | Intended safe rejection |
77	| Real or false-positive semantic breakage | N294–312: no verdict, `VERDICT: BREAKAGE`, rate limit, context exhaustion, or reviewer failure | Low/medium depending delta and service | Intended safe rejection; may require manual recovery |
78	| Claude/npm availability or CLI drift | Unpinned `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` at N187 and N284; Claude calls at N192/N294 | Low per run; cumulative material | Safe retry for outage/quota; persistent if CLI/model contract changes |
79	| Fresh dependency blocked by age gate | N256 `bun install --lockfile-only`; [bunfig.toml](</C:/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64/bunfig.toml:4>) sets `minimumReleaseAge = 259200` | Medium when upstream pins a newly published package | Safe; generally succeeds after up to 72 hours. `resolve-release-age.mjs` is not invoked here. |
80	| Manifest/override/lock incompatibility | N249 or N256 fails. The override checker intentionally misses ranges/protocols, peer-only and purely transitive mismatches. | Low | Safe; blind spots surface later at B109 or B240 |
81	| Windows-only install/build drift | B109 frozen install, B119 reference gate, B124 icons, B129 compile | Low/medium per meaningful release | Safe persistent blocker until source/build compatibility exists |
82	| Missing companion native release | B160–179 requires exact-version libsql and tokenizers releases | Medium when either dependency bumps | Explicit `No ... Release for ...`; safe but repeats until external artifacts exist |
83	| Electron/better-sqlite ABI lag | B193–205 and M60–68 require a known Electron ABI and exact Windows ARM64 better-sqlite3 prebuild | Medium eventually | `Unknown Electron major`, HTTP 404, or native validation failure; safe persistent blocker |
84	| Bun-store selection picks wrong version | B156, B171, B190–191 select the highest matching store directory, not necessarily the desktop consumer’s instance | Low, increasing with multiple versions | Usually safe missing-artifact/ABI abort; theoretically can select a compatible-looking wrong native |
85	| Static build/output assumptions change | B137–148 requires `config-*` and `terminal-runtime-*`; B249–260 requires `win-arm64-unpacked`, `Superset.exe`, and a fixed tokenizers path | Low per release | Safe false-abort after a relevant upstream refactor |
86	| Packaging/native regression | B216, B227, B240, B249–270 | Low/medium | Safe intended abort |
87	| Windows ARM runner/toolchain outage or image drift | B37 uses `windows-11-arm`, still Public Preview and “as available” | Low/medium transiently | Safe retry unless loss occurs during publishing. GitHub has also announced migration of this label to the VS2026 image in early September 2026, creating a concrete future toolchain-change risk. [Runner reference](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/runners/github-hosted-runners), [runner migration notice](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-06-11-new-runner-images-in-public-preview/) |
88	| Candidate push/network failure | N342 may delete the old branch, then N343 fails to create the replacement | Low | Baseline/release untouched; next run retries cleanly |
89	
90	## Non-transactional half-states that normally self-heal
91	
92	- B298–299 destroys the current release and tag before B306 establishes the replacement. API/upload/runner failure can leave no public release, a draft/tag, or a partially completed operation. `gh release create TAG ASSET` uses separate draft, upload, and publish operations. [GitHub CLI release behavior](https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_release_create)
93	- B311 `git ls-remote` can fail after publication, leaving the new release present while the build reports red and `main` remains old.
94	- N388 can fail after publication, leaving the release at the candidate. A normal next nightly repairs this because the baseline is still old.
95	- N388 can succeed and N389 candidate deletion can fail. The run reports red even though baseline and release are complete; the stale candidate remains, but future versions are not wedged.
96	
97	These become permanent only when combined with the stale-queued-run race, immutable releases, protection rules, or an external publisher race.
98	
99	## False-green integrity gaps
100	
101	These do not make the nightly fail; they can let it succeed incorrectly.
102	
103	- The marker gate passes vacuously if the fenced ` ```markers` opening is removed or the block becomes empty. It asserts no minimum marker count.
104	- F60 lists the Auto-resume feature markers—`(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, and `AutoResumeController`—but none appears in the machine-readable block at F74–138. That feature currently has no deterministic marker protection.
105	- Marker presence only proves a token exists somewhere under a broad root; dead/commented/unreachable code passes.
106	- Resolver scope is prompt-only. It can edit non-conflicted, unstaged, or untracked files; the reviewer is instructed to inspect `git diff --cached`, while N329 ultimately commits everything via `git add -A`.
107	- N289/N299 hashes `git status --porcelain`, not file contents. Editing and restaging an already-staged path can leave the same porcelain status and evade the “review changed nothing” assertion.
108	- N294 ignores Claude’s exit status. If the reviewer writes `VERDICT: OK` first and then crashes or times out, N296 accepts the partial verdict.
109	- Dropped upstream workflow changes produce advisory `CI-NOTE:` lines only. They never change the verdict, so a newly required codegen/environment/package step can be omitted if the build does not expose it.
110	- R ignores nonmatching `tsc` failures and skips missing `tsconfig.json` paths. B269’s process-substitution `find` failure does not propagate, and the native scan does not reject every generically named x64 `.node`.
111	- B279–280 publishes the first matching `.exe`; multiple output executables can select the wrong asset without failing.
112	- There is no full test/e2e gate, an accepted fork limitation.
113	- Manual `force_tag` is inserted directly into shell source at N96: `FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'`. A quote-bearing dispatch input can break or inject shell. Similarly, B292–293 interpolates package/output values into shell source. Normal upstream semver and generated filenames are safe, but these API boundaries are unvalidated.
114	
115	## Permission and handoff verdict
116	
117	The normal token boundary is correct:
118	
119	- `contents: write` is sufficient for branch, tag, and release operations.
120	- The reusable build cannot elevate beyond the caller, but the caller already grants the same permission.
121	- Build and advance consume the immutable merge SHA, not the mutable candidate branch.
122	- `gh release create --target "$BUILT_SHA"` and B311–315 correctly verify the tag.
123	- The workflow-tree restore closes the known `GITHUB_TOKEN` workflow-file push rejection for ordinary final-tree changes. GitHub separately requires a Workflows permission to edit workflow files, which the standard Actions token cannot request through workflow `permissions`. [GitHub App workflow permission](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/creating-github-apps/registering-a-github-app/choosing-permissions-for-a-github-app), [reusable-workflow permissions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/reusing-workflow-configurations)
124	
125	Historical bot pushes prove the required main/release permissions worked through July 11. Current live rulesets and immutable-release settings could not be queried from this sandbox, so the configuration-dependent classes above cannot be ruled out from repository text alone.
126	
127	## Final answer
128	
129	For ordinary stable upstream releases, yes: the pipeline is now reasonably expected to succeed unattended, and the specific v1.15.0 workflow modify/delete failure should be gone.
130	
131	But “whenever upstream publishes any new `desktop-v*` tag” is false:
132	
133	- prereleases are deliberately ignored;
134	- fresh dependencies can delay success up to 72 hours;
135	- native dependency bumps can require companion artifacts;
136	- real semantic or build incompatibilities intentionally stop for recovery;
137	- Claude, registries, GitHub, and the preview ARM runner remain live dependencies;
138	- low-probability queued-run, concurrent-publisher, and version-mismatch cases can leave a wrong release that future no-op nightlies do not repair.
139	
140	The most likely remaining reds are safe dependency-age, service, merge/semantic, native-artifact, or build-drift aborts. The most dangerous residuals are the stale queued-event-SHA race, a concurrent standalone publisher, and the missing `NEWTAG`↔package-version invariant.
141	
142	No files were modified; the worktree remained clean.
